# Test basic Puppeteer server functionality with aggressive timeouts

# Set aggressive environment variables (Puppeteer needs more time for browser startup)
env MCP_TEST_TIMEOUT=5s
env MCP_SERVER_TIMEOUT=3s
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=4s

# Start Puppeteer server
bg bash 'go run main.go'
sleep 2s

# Test list_tools capability
bash '
echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" | go run main.go
'
stdout '"name": "navigate"'
stdout '"name": "screenshot"'
stdout '"name": "get_content"'

# Test navigate tool
bash '
echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 2, \"method\": \"tools/call\", \"params\": {\"name\": \"navigate\", \"arguments\": {\"url\": \"https://example.com\"}}}" | timeout 8s go run main.go
'
stdout 'navigated'

# Test get_content tool  
bash '
echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 3, \"method\": \"tools/call\", \"params\": {\"name\": \"get_content\", \"arguments\": {\"url\": \"https://example.com\"}}}" | timeout 8s go run main.go
'
stdout 'Example Domain'

# Test screenshot tool
bash '
echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 4, \"method\": \"tools/call\", \"params\": {\"name\": \"screenshot\", \"arguments\": {\"url\": \"https://example.com\"}}}" | timeout 10s go run main.go
'
stdout 'screenshot'

# Test server response time (should complete reasonably fast)
bash 'start_time=$(date +%s%N); echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 5, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" | go run main.go > /dev/null; end_time=$(date +%s%N); duration=$((($end_time - $start_time) / 1000000)); echo "Response time: ${duration}ms"; test $duration -lt 3000'

# Cleanup
exec pkill -f "go run main.go" || true
